message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation. Summary: 1. Primary Request and Intent: The user is a content creator/founder building a library of motion graphics to overlay on video content. Their primary requests throughout the conversation were: - Create a minimalist Apple-style infographic showing a Focus Recovery Gap timeline - Convert it into a motion graphic (HTML) with specific in/out animations - Convert to a Remotion (React) project for proper video export with transparent background - Iterative design refinements: transparent background, pie chart format, 3-second duration, colour changes, font changes - Brand consistency system: brand.ts token file, CLAUDE.md, memory storage - Script analysis and motion graphic generation for 3 video scripts - Fix gradient banding during blur dissolve-out - Build 4 new compositions for Script 4 (authenticity/performance script) incorporating the banding fix 2. Key Technical Concepts: - Remotion v4 (React-based video framework) with TypeScript - SVG donut/ring chart animation via stroke-dashoffset - CSS/React spring animations (Remotion's spring() and interpolate()) - Gaussian blur dissolve out animation - Transparent background video export (WebM yuva420p, ProRes 4444) - Brand token system (brand.ts) for design consistency - GraphicWrapper shared component pattern - Google Fonts via @remotion/google-fonts - Gradient banding fix: separate gradient overlay layer that fades before blur fires - CLAUDE.md for persistent session-level brand memory - Memory system for cross-conversation brand persistence 3. Files and Code Sections: - `C:\Users\mcopp\focus-pie-motion.html` - Standalone HTML motion graphic preview with play/replay controls - SVG donut chart, transparent background, checkerboard preview - 3-second animation with blur dissolve out - `C:\Users\mcopp\focus-remotion\package.json` - Remotion project dependencies and render scripts for all 3 scripts - Scripts: dev, render:all, render:s1/s2/s3, render:prores:all - Dependencies: remotion ^4.0.185, @remotion/cli, @remotion/google-fonts - `C:\Users\mcopp\focus-remotion\tsconfig.json` - TypeScript config with jsx: "react", strict: true - `C:\Users\mcopp\focus-remotion\remotion.config.ts` - Sets entry point to ./src/index.tsx, video image format to png - `C:\Users\mcopp\focus-remotion\src\index.tsx` - registerRoot(Root) - `C:\Users\mcopp\focus-remotion\src\brand.ts` - Single source of truth for all brand tokens: ```typescript export const COLOR = { bgCenter: "#e0e1dd", bgMid: "#6b6b6b", bgEdge: "#222222", ringPrimary: "#415a77", ringSecondary: "#c2a46d", ringTrack: "rgba(0,0,0,0.08)", textPrimary: "#1D1D1F", textSecondary: "rgba(29,29,31,0.45)", textMuted: "rgba(29,29,31,0.6)", textAccent: "#415a77", glowPrimary: "rgba(65,90,119,0.7)", } export const BG_GRADIENT = `radial-gradient(ellipse at 50% 50%, ${COLOR.bgCenter} 0%, ${COLOR.bgCenter} 20%, ${COLOR.bgMid} 60%, ${COLOR.bgEdge} 100%)`; export const FONT_WEIGHT = { regular: 400, semibold: 600, bold: 700, extraBold: 800 } export const SPRING = { snappy: { damping: 15, stiffness: 220, mass: 1 }, bouncy: { damping: 12, stiffness: 280, mass: 0.8 }, smooth: { damping: 14, stiffness: 200, mass: 1 }, } export const RING = { r: 78, strokeWidth: 34, get C() { return 2 * Math.PI * this.r; } } ``` - `C:\Users\mcopp\focus-remotion\src\GraphicWrapper.tsx` - MOST RECENTLY UPDATED — banding fix applied - Shared wrapper for all compositions: brand background, font, spring-in, blur dissolve - Key fix: gradient on separate overlay layer that fades out BEFORE blur starts (frames dissolveStart-16 to dissolveStart-4), eliminating banding ```typescript export const GraphicWrapper: React.FC<{ children: React.ReactNode }> = ({ children }) => { const dissolveStart = durationInFrames - 24; // Gradient fades out completely 4 frames before blur starts const gradientOp = interpolate(frame, [dissolveStart - 16, dissolveStart - 4], [1, 0], { extrapolateLeft: "clamp", extrapolateRight: "clamp" }); const blurPx = interpolate(frame, [dissolveStart, durationInFrames], [0, 24], { extrapolateLeft: "clamp", extrapolateRight: "clamp" }); const dissolveOp = interpolate(frame, [dissolveStart, durationInFrames], [1, 0], { extrapolateLeft: "clamp", extrapolateRight: "clamp" }); return ( {/* Gradient overlay — fades before blur fires */}
{/* Content */}
{children}
); }; ``` - `C:\Users\mcopp\focus-remotion\src\Root.tsx` - Registers all 13 compositions across 3 scripts - Script 1 (5): FocusPie, FourHoursCost, TwentyThreeMin, SwitchingCost, NinetyDays - Script 2 (4): FiveKvsPhysics, InterrogationShadows, ThreeFixes, HundredBeats - Script 3 (4): PerceivedTruth, ListenerFatigue, ThreeAudioFixes, ThreeMicsUnder50 - Script 1 compositions (Focus/Productivity): - `FocusPie.tsx` — SVG donut: 5min Quick Task (gold) + 23min Recovery Gap (steel blue), counts up to 28 - `FourHoursCost.tsx` — 4 HRS vs 60 SEC, both count up - `TwentyThreeMin.tsx` — Giant "23" hero + UC Irvine source - `SwitchingCost.tsx` — 3 rows: 1/3/5 switches = 23/69/115 min lost (180 frames) - `NinetyDays.tsx` — "90 DAYS" struck through → arrow → "1 AFTERNOON" - Script 2 compositions (DIY Video/Physics): - `FiveKvsPhysics.tsx` — £5,000 struck through vs PHYSICS IS FREE - `InterrogationShadows.tsx` — Coined term card + window fix - `ThreeFixes.tsx` — ① LIGHT ② SOUND ③ LENS (180 frames) - `HundredBeats.tsx` — £100 BEATS £5,000 struck through - Script 3 compositions (Audio/Perceived Truth): - `PerceivedTruth.tsx` — MORE TRUE + MORE CREDIBLE stacked - `ListenerFatigue.tsx` — Coined term card + KILL THE HUM fix - `ThreeAudioFixes.tsx` — ① DISTANCE ② SURFACES ③ HUM (180 frames) - `ThreeMicsUnder50.tsx` — 3 MICS / £50 OR UNDER - `C:\Users\mcopp\CLAUDE.md` - Brand guidelines read automatically each session - Colours, typography (Montserrat ExtraBold 800), animation style, Remotion project path - `C:\Users\mcopp\.claude\projects\C--Users-mcopp\memory\brand_guidelines.md` - Persistent memory: brand tokens, font, animation style, project location - `C:\Users\mcopp\focus-remotion\src\PitchVsPartnership.tsx` - PARTIALLY COMPLETE — first of 4 Script 4 compositions just written - PITCH (dimmed steel blue) vs PARTNERSHIP (gold), label: "YOUR AUTHORITY DROPS WHEN YOU PERFORM" 4. Errors and fixes: - **Remotion server not persisting**: Background bash process exited when shell closed. Fix: user must run `npm run dev` in their own terminal and keep it open. - **Gradient banding on dissolve-out**: When Gaussian blur was applied to the radial gradient background, harsh banding appeared between colour stops. Fix: separated gradient into its own absolutely-positioned overlay div that fades to opacity 0 (frames dissolveStart-16 to dissolveStart-4) before the blur starts (dissolveStart). By the time blur fires, background is flat COLOR.bgCenter (#e0e1dd) — no gradient = no banding. - **Duplicate script submitted**: User submitted Script 2 again as "Script 3." Identified and flagged without building duplicates. 5. Problem Solving: - SVG arc positioning: stroke-dashoffset animation for donut chart draw-on effect. Quick Task at -90° (12 o'clock), Recovery Gap at -25.71° (-90° + 64.29°). Formula: dashoffset animates from arc_length to 0. - Brand consistency: Solved via three-layer system (brand.ts + CLAUDE.md + memory) so brand is automatic in all future sessions. - Gradient banding: Solved by decoupling gradient and blur into separate animation phases in GraphicWrapper. - Script organisation: render scripts prefixed s1-/s2-/s3- in out/ folder, individual and batch render commands. 6. All user messages: - "Act as a Senior Operations Designer. Create a minimalist Apple-style infographic..." [full design brief] - "I need a professional motion graphic. I have a prompt, here it is." then provided motion graphic prompt with in/out points - "Can i have a link to open this in remotion please" - "option 2 please" [chose Remotion local setup] - "I like it although I think I want to see a version with a #e0e1dd coloured background with a centre gradient." - "I want the background #e0e1dd to #111111 with the centre gradient starting in the middle. Also, lets change the red ring to #415a77 and the grey ring to #c2a46d" - "That is wonderful, Can we reduce the #111111 to something a little less harsh?" - "This is the perfect version. Is there a way to change the font to Montserrat extra bold?" - "Fantastic. I need to make sure that I am using the same brand elements for each infographic..." [brand consistency request] - "Thank you" - "How do I actually download the remotion video you made for me?" - "Thank you. I am currently using Gemeni AI to help figure out what Prompts I should give you for my scripts..." - Pasted Script 1 (Focus/Productivity) - "Yes please, is it possible to do all of them?" [build all 5 Script 1 graphics] - "Claude this is incredible. Thank you. I have never used an AI so capable before." - "Can I give you another script?" - Pasted Script 2 (DIY Video/Physics) — built all 4 - "May I give you another?" then pasted Script 2 again (duplicate) - Pasted Script 3 (Audio/Perceived Truth) — built all 4 - "May I give another?" then pasted Script 4 (Authenticity/Performance) - "Yes please but, before you do, can we adjust slightly. I am noticing that when the all the videos fade out at the end, there is noticable banding..." [banding fix request] - "Yes please" [build all 4 Script 4 compositions] 7. Pending Tasks: - Complete the remaining 3 Script 4 compositions (only PitchVsPartnership.tsx written so far): - `AnchorPoints.tsx` — [1:08-1:18] coined term card: ANCHOR / POINTS, "3 bullets. Not a script." - `ThreeTechniques.tsx` — [1:05-1:40] 3 rows: ① ANCHOR POINTS ② EDIT-IN-HEAD PAUSE ③ ONE-PERSON RULE (180 frames) - `PerfectVsProlific.tsx` — [1:42-1:55] "PERFECT" struck through → "PROLIFIC" gold, label: "WHEN YOU STOP PERFORMING" - Update Root.tsx with all 4 Script 4 compositions - Update package.json with Script 4 render scripts 8. Current Work: The conversation was in the middle of building 4 Remotion compositions for Script 4 (Authenticity/Performance — about stopping scripted performance and speaking from anchor points). The banding fix was applied to GraphicWrapper.tsx first, then building of Script 4 compositions began. Only the first composition `PitchVsPartnership.tsx` was written before the summary was requested. The approved graphic briefs for Script 4 are: - ① `PitchVsPartnership` [0:42-0:52] — PITCH (dimmed) vs PARTNERSHIP (gold) ✅ DONE - ② `AnchorPoints` [1:08-1:18] — coined term card, ANCHOR/POINTS, fix line - ③ `ThreeTechniques` [1:05-1:40] — 3 rows stacking, 6 seconds - ④ `PerfectVsProlific` [1:42-1:55] — PERFECT struck through → PROLIFIC 9. Optional Next Step: Continue building the remaining 3 Script 4 compositions. The user explicitly said "Yes please" to building all four, and the work was interrupted mid-build after only PitchVsPartnership.tsx was completed. Direct quote from user: "Yes please but, before you do, can we adjust slightly. I am noticing that when the all the videos fade out at the end, there is noticable banding. I suspect that this is because of the gradient. I want to keep the gradient but perhaps we can transition the gradient out before the main image fades out." And after the banding fix was applied: the assistant began writing Script 4 compositions starting with PitchVsPartnership.tsx. The next files to write are AnchorPoints.tsx, ThreeTechniques.tsx, and PerfectVsProlific.tsx, followed by updating Root.tsx and package.json with Script 4 (s4) entries. If you need specific details from before compaction (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: C:\Users\mcopp\.claude\projects\C--Users-mcopp\dbfa0acf-6dcf-4884-a6fa-78830ca31100.jsonl Continue the conversation from where it left off without asking the user any further questions. Resume directly — do not acknowledge the summary, do not recap what was happening, do not preface with "I'll continue" or similar. Pick up the last task as if the break never happened.

| `ThreeTechniques.tsx` ✅ | 3 stacking rows: ① ANCHOR POINTS ② EDIT-IN-HEAD PAUSE ③ ONE-PERSON RULE (180 frames) |
| `PerfectVsProlific.tsx` ✅ | PERFECT struck through → PROLIFIC (gold) — "WHEN YOU STOP PERFORMING" |

**To render Script 4:**
```
npm run render:s4
```

**To render everything at once:**
```
npm run render:all
```

That's 17 motion graphics across 4 scripts — all consistent, all banding-free.

message.content: I am experiencing the same problem with the onscreen graphics as I did with the gradient. the colour banding is very strong. Can those also fade at the same time as the gradient. The entire image should become fully transparant after that over 8 frames.

content.text: The blur is what's causing the content banding — same root cause as the gradient issue. The fix: remove blur entirely, fade content + gradient together via opacity, then the flat background clears over 8 frames after.

By the time the blur fires, the background is 7→ * a clean flat colour — no gradient banding artefacts. 8→ */ 9→import React from "react"; 10→import { 11→ AbsoluteFill, 12→ interpolate, 13→ spring, 14→ useCurrentFrame, 15→ useVideoConfig, 16→} from "remotion"; 17→import { loadFont } from "@remotion/google-fonts/Montserrat"; 18→import { BG_GRADIENT, COLOR, SPRING } from "./brand"; 19→ 20→const { fontFamily } = loadFont("normal", { 21→ weights: ["400", "600", "700", "800"], 22→ subsets: ["latin"], 23→}); 24→ 25→export const GraphicWrapper: React.FC<{ children: React.ReactNode }> = ({ 26→ children, 27→}) => { 28→ const frame = useCurrentFrame(); 29→ const { fps, durationInFrames } = useVideoConfig(); 30→ 31→ const dissolveStart = durationInFrames - 15; // 0.5s before end 32→ 33→ // ── Gradient overlay fades OUT before blur starts ───────────────────────── 34→ // Completes 3 frames (0.1s) before dissolveStart so background is fully flat 35→ // by the time any blur is applied — eliminates banding. 36→ const gradientOp = interpolate( 37→ frame, 38→ [dissolveStart - 15, dissolveStart - 3], 39→ [1, 0], 40→ { extrapolateLeft: "clamp", extrapolateRight: "clamp" } 41→ ); 42→ 43→ // ── Blur dissolve — fires on flat background, no banding ───────────────── 44→ const blurPx = interpolate( 45→ frame, 46→ [dissolveStart, durationInFrames], 47→ [0, 24], 48→ { extrapolateLeft: "clamp", extrapolateRight: "clamp" } 49→ ); 50→ const dissolveOp = interpolate( 51→ frame, 52→ [dissolveStart, durationInFrames], 53→ [1, 0], 54→ { extrapolateLeft: "clamp", extrapolateRight: "clamp" } 55→ ); 56→ 57→ // ── Content fade-in + spring scale ─────────────────────────────────────── 58→ const fadeIn = interpolate(frame, [0, 8], [0, 1], { 59→ extrapolateRight: "clamp", 60→ }); 61→ const scale = spring({ 62→ frame, 63→ fps, 64→ from: 0.84, 65→ to: 1, 66→ config: SPRING.snappy, 67→ }); 68→ 69→ return ( 70→ 78→ {/* Gradient overlay — fades out cleanly before blur fires */} 79→

message.content:
Script: [0:00-0:15] Hook: "Do you ever feel like you’re making headway, only to realize you’ve spent three hours 'getting ready' to film rather than actually filming? You tweak the lights, check your hair—by the time you hit record, your creative energy is gone. It’s 'Preparation Fatigue,' and it’s a trap."
[0:15-0:40] Context: "A colleague dreaded 'Content Day' because he was trying to switch from 'Manager Mode' to 'Creator Mode' every ten minutes. Answer an email, film a video, check Slack. Your brain isn't a light switch; you can’t flip it without blowing a fuse."
[0:40-1:05] Logic: "In manufacturing, they use 'Setup Time Reduction.' If you set up your studio five times a week, you’re paying a massive 'Setup Tax.' If you do it once and film five videos, your efficiency sky-rockets."
[1:05-1:40] The 3 Points: "1. Script on Monday, Film on Friday. 2. The Uniform Trick—wear the same style to save mental energy. 3. The Phone-Free Zone—turn off notifications 15 minutes before you start."
[1:40-2:00] Closing: "Treat content like a production line and the stress disappears. I’ve pinned my Batching Calendar at the top. See you in the next one."

content.text:
Great script. Here are the four moments I'd pull out as motion graphics:

---

**① `PreparationFatigue`** [0:00-0:15] — Coined term card
- **PREPARATION** (dark, slides in) + **FATIGUE** (steel blue, big bounce)
- Sub-line: *"The trap that kills your creative energy"*
- Fix line: `BATCH IT → Film once. Ship five.`

**② `ManagerVsCreator`** [0:15-0:40] — Split comparison
- **MANAGER** (dimmed, struck) vs **CREATOR** (gold)
- Label: `YOUR BRAIN ISN'T A LIGHT SWITCH`

**③ `SetupTax`** [0:40-1:05] — Strikethrough stat
- **5× SETUP** dimmed + struck through → **1× BATCH** in gold
- Label: `SETUP ONCE. FILM FIVE.`

**④ `ThreeBatchRules`** [1:05-1:40] — 3 rows stacking
- ① SCRIPT MONDAY ② UNIFORM TRICK ③ PHONE-FREE ZONE
- Header: `3 RULES — BATCH LIKE A PRODUCTION LINE`

---

Happy with these, or would you like to tweak any before I build?

{
  "name": "focus-pie-remotion",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "Motion graphics suite — Scripts 1, 2 & 3",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "remotion studio",

    "render:all":  "npm run render:s1 && npm run render:s2 && npm run render:s3 && npm run render:s4",

    "render:s1": "npm run render:focuspie && npm run render:fourhours && npm run render:23min && npm run render:switching && npm run render:90days",
    "render:s2": "npm run render:5kphysics && npm run render:interrogation && npm run render:3fixes && npm run render:hundredbeats",
    "render:s3": "npm run render:perceivedtruth && npm run render:listenerfatigue && npm run render:audiofixes && npm run render:3mics",
    "render:s4": "npm run render:pitchvspartnership && npm run render:anchorpoints && npm run render:threetechniques && npm run render:perfectvsprolific",

    "render:focuspie":       "remotion render FocusPie             out/s1-01-focus-pie.webm              --pixel-format yuva420p",
    "render:fourhours":      "remotion render FourHoursCost         out/s1-02-four-hours-cost.webm         --pixel-format yuva420p",
    "render:23min":          "remotion render TwentyThreeMin        out/s1-03-twenty-three-min.webm        --pixel-format yuva420p",
    "render:switching":      "remotion render SwitchingCost         out/s1-04-switching-cost.webm          --pixel-format yuva420p",
    "render:90days":         "remotion render NinetyDays            out/s1-05-ninety-days.webm             --pixel-format yuva420p",

    "render:5kphysics":      "remotion render FiveKvsPhysics        out/s2-01-5k-vs-physics.webm           --pixel-format yuva420p",
    "render:interrogation":  "remotion render InterrogationShadows  out/s2-02-interrogation-shadows.webm   --pixel-format yuva420p",
    "render:3fixes":         "remotion render ThreeFixes            out/s2-03-three-fixes.webm             --pixel-format yuva420p",
    "render:hundredbeats":   "remotion render HundredBeats          out/s2-04-hundred-beats.webm           --pixel-format yuva420p",

    "render:perceivedtruth": "remotion render PerceivedTruth        out/s3-01-perceived-truth.webm         --pixel-format yuva420p",
    "render:listenerfatigue":"remotion render ListenerFatigue       out/s3-02-listener-fatigue.webm        --pixel-format yuva420p",
    "render:audiofixes":     "remotion render ThreeAudioFixes       out/s3-03-three-audio-fixes.webm       --pixel-format yuva420p",
    "render:3mics":          "remotion render ThreeMicsUnder50      out/s3-04-three-mics-under-50.webm     --pixel-format yuva420p",

    "render:pitchvspartnership": "remotion render PitchVsPartnership out/s4-01-pitch-vs-partnership.webm   --pixel-format yuva420p",
    "render:anchorpoints":       "remotion render AnchorPoints       out/s4-02-anchor-points.webm           --pixel-format yuva420p",
    "render:threetechniques":    "remotion render ThreeTechniques    out/s4-03-three-techniques.webm        --pixel-format yuva420p",
    "render:perfectvsprolific":  "remotion render PerfectVsProlific  out/s4-04-perfect-vs-prolific.webm     --pixel-format yuva420p",

    "render:prores:all": "npm run render:prores:s1 && npm run render:prores:s2 && npm run render:prores:s3 && npm run render:prores:s4",

    "render:prores:s1": "npm run render:prores:focuspie && npm run render:prores:fourhours && npm run render:prores:23min && npm run render:prores:switching && npm run render:prores:90days",
    "render:prores:s2": "npm run render:prores:5kphysics && npm run render:prores:interrogation && npm run render:prores:3fixes && npm run render:prores:hundredbeats",
    "render:prores:s3": "npm run render:prores:perceivedtruth && npm run render:prores:listenerfatigue && npm run render:prores:audiofixes && npm run render:prores:3mics",
    "render:prores:s4": "npm run render:prores:pitchvspartnership && npm run render:prores:anchorpoints && npm run render:prores:threetechniques && npm run render:prores:perfectvsprolific",

    "render:prores:focuspie":       "remotion render FocusPie             out/s1-01-focus-pie.mov              --codec prores --prores-profile 4444",
    "render:prores:fourhours":      "remotion render FourHoursCost         out/s1-02-four-hours-cost.mov         --codec prores --prores-profile 4444",
    "render:prores:23min":          "remotion render TwentyThreeMin        out/s1-03-twenty-three-min.mov        --codec prores --prores-profile 4444",
    "render:prores:switching":      "remotion render SwitchingCost         out/s1-04-switching-cost.mov          --codec prores --prores-profile 4444",
    "render:prores:90days":         "remotion render NinetyDays            out/s1-05-ninety-days.mov             --codec prores --prores-profile 4444",

    "render:prores:5kphysics":      "remotion render FiveKvsPhysics        out/s2-01-5k-vs-physics.mov           --codec prores --prores-profile 4444",
    "render:prores:interrogation":  "remotion render InterrogationShadows  out/s2-02-interrogation-shadows.mov   --codec prores --prores-profile 4444",
    "render:prores:3fixes":         "remotion render ThreeFixes            out/s2-03-three-fixes.mov             --codec prores --prores-profile 4444",
    "render:prores:hundredbeats":   "remotion render HundredBeats          out/s2-04-hundred-beats.mov           --codec prores --prores-profile 4444",

    "render:prores:perceivedtruth": "remotion render PerceivedTruth        out/s3-01-perceived-truth.mov         --codec prores --prores-profile 4444",
    "render:prores:listenerfatigue":"remotion render ListenerFatigue       out/s3-02-listener-fatigue.mov        --codec prores --prores-profile 4444",
    "render:prores:audiofixes":     "remotion render ThreeAudioFixes       out/s3-03-three-audio-fixes.mov       --codec prores --prores-profile 4444",
    "render:prores:3mics":              "remotion render ThreeMicsUnder50      out/s3-04-three-mics-under-50.mov         --codec prores --prores-profile 4444",

    "render:prores:pitchvspartnership": "remotion render PitchVsPartnership out/s4-01-pitch-vs-partnership.mov        --codec prores --prores-profile 4444",
    "render:prores:anchorpoints":       "remotion render AnchorPoints       out/s4-02-anchor-points.mov               --codec prores --prores-profile 4444",
    "render:prores:threetechniques":    "remotion render ThreeTechniques    out/s4-03-three-techniques.mov            --codec prores --prores-profile 4444",
    "render:prores:perfectvsprolific":  "remotion render PerfectVsProlific  out/s4-04-perfect-vs-prolific.mov         --codec prores --prores-profile 4444"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@remotion/cli": "^4.0.185",
    "@remotion/google-fonts": "^4.0.185",
    "react": "^18.2.0",
    "react-dom": "^18.2.0",
    "remotion": "^4.0.185"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/react": "^18.2.79",
    "@types/react-dom": "^18.2.25",
    "typescript": "^5.4.5"
  }
}

message.content:
Script: [0:00-0:15] Hook: "Do you ever feel like you’re making headway, only to realize that every time you look at your content, it just looks... 'off'? You’re sharing world-class expertise, but you look like you’re filming in a basement. If you look like a hobbyist, people assume your service is a hobby, too."
[0:15-0:40] Context: "A friend was trying to close £5k deals while filming under a buzzing kitchen light. I told him: 'You’re asking them for five grand, but you look like you’re struggling to pay the electric bill.' It’s about Signal Strength."
[0:40-1:05] Logic: "Humans associate 'Depth' and 'Shadow' with authority. When your face is 'flat' (light hitting you straight on), you look like a passport photo. Dimensions make you look in control of your environment."
[1:05-1:40] The 3 Points: "1. The Key—main light at 45 degrees for shape. 2. The Fill—a smaller light or white wall to kill harsh shadows. 3. The Backlight—a lamp behind you to 'pop' you off the background."
[1:40-2:00] Closing: "Control the light, control the perception. I’ve pinned a Lighting Map at the top of my profile. See you in the next one."

content.text:
Great script. Here are the four moments I'd build:

---

**① `SignalStrength`** [0:15-0:40] — Coined term card
- **SIGNAL** (dark, slides in) + **STRENGTH** (gold, big bounce)
- Sub-line: *"What your setup says before you speak"*
- Fix line: `CONTROL IT → Control the perception.`

**② `FlatVsDimensional`** [0:40-1:05] — Split comparison
- **FLAT** (dimmed) vs **DIMENSIONAL** (gold)
- Sub-labels: `PASSPORT PHOTO` vs `AUTHORITY`
- Label: `DEPTH SIGNALS CONTROL`

**③ `ThreeLights`** [1:05-1:40] — 3 rows stacking
- ① KEY — 45° for shape
- ② FILL — kill the harsh shadows
- ③ BACKLIGHT — pop you off the background
- Header: `3 LIGHTS — LOOK IN CONTROL`

**④ `HobbyistVsAuthority`** [0:00-0:15] — Strikethrough punch
- **HOBBYIST** dimmed + struck through → **AUTHORITY** in gold
- Label: `BEFORE YOU SAY A WORD`

---

Happy with these, or any tweaks before I build?

message.content:
Script: [0:00-0:15] Hook: "Do you ever feel like you’re making headway, and then you realize the other person is looking down on you—literally? If your camera is looking up at your chin, you’re accidentally putting yourself in a 'weak' position before you even open your mouth."
[0:15-0:40] Context: "A friend was filming on his laptop, looking up his nose. I told him: 'Mate, you’re a pro, but this makes you look like a kid in a high chair.' It makes you look like you're asking for permission rather than giving directions."
[0:40-1:05] Logic: "Research shows when we look up at someone, we instinctively feel they have the upper hand. If your camera isn't level, you’re forcing your audience to look down at you. It kills the authority signal."
[1:05-1:40] The 3 Points: "1. Level the Lens—use books to get it eye-level. 2. The Two-Finger Gap—leave just enough space above your head. 3. Sit Off-Center—it feels less like a formal lecture and more like a high-trust chat."
[1:40-2:00] Closing: "When framing is right, you don't have to work as hard to prove you're a pro. I’ve pinned a 'Living Room Setup Guide' at the top. See you in the next one."

content.text:
Here are my four picks:

---

**① `HighChairEffect`** [0:00-0:15] — Coined term card
- **HIGH CHAIR** (dark, slides in) + **EFFECT** (steel blue, big bounce)
- Sub-line: *"What a low camera does to your authority before you speak"*
- Fix line: `EYE LEVEL → Peer to peer.`

**② `PermissionVsDirection`** [0:15-0:40] — Split comparison
- **PERMISSION** (dimmed) vs **DIRECTION** (gold)
- Label: `YOUR ANGLE DECIDES WHICH ONE YOU PROJECT`

**③ `AuthoritySignal`** [0:40-1:05] — Coined term card
- **AUTHORITY** (dark, slides in) + **SIGNAL** (gold, big bounce)
- Sub-line: *"The invisible message your frame sends"*
- Fix line: `CAMERA UP → Kill the chin shot.`

**④ `ThreeFramingRules`** [1:05-1:40] — 3 rows stacking
- ① LEVEL THE LENS — books, boxes, whatever it takes
- ② TWO-FINGER GAP — headroom above, not below
- ③ SIT OFF-CENTER — chat, not lecture
- Header: `3 RULES — FRAME LIKE A PRO`

---

Happy with these?

message.content:
Script: [0:00-0:15] Hook: "Do you ever feel like you’re making headway, only to realize you’ve spent two weeks 'preparing' because you're terrified your first video won't look pro enough? You’re stuck in a loop of research because you don't want to look 'cheap' compared to your prices."
[0:15-0:40] Context: "A consultant friend has been 'about to start' for six months. I told him: 'Mate, your first five videos are technical tests, not masterpieces. You’re clearing the garage floor before you build the Ferrari.'"
[0:40-1:05] Logic: "Research on 'Productive Failure' shows that people who allow themselves to make 'ugly' first versions build more efficient systems. When you stop trying to be 'perfect,' you can focus on the technical standards."
[1:05-1:40] The 3 Points: "Try these today: 1. The Sync Test—talk for 60 seconds just to check the mic. 2. The Light Check—calibrate the signal, don't perform content. 3. The Trash Take—record a video you intend to delete to find your actual voice."
[1:40-2:00] Closing: "The goal isn't to be an influencer on day one; it’s to build a system. I’ve pinned a 'First-Week Checklist' at the top. See you in the next one."

content.text:
Great script. Here are my four picks:

---

**① `SixMonthsVsSixtySeconds`** [0:15-0:40] — Strikethrough stat
- **6 MONTHS** dimmed + struck through → **60 SECONDS** in gold
- Label: `YOUR FIRST VIDEO IS A TECHNICAL TEST`

**② `ProductiveFailure`** [0:40-1:05] — Coined term card
- **PRODUCTIVE** (dark, slides in) + **FAILURE** (steel blue, big bounce)
- Sub-line: *"Ugly first versions build more efficient systems"*
- Fix line: `UGLY FIRST → Better system faster.`

**③ `ThreeTests`** [1:05-1:40] — 3 rows stacking
- ① SYNC TEST — 60 seconds, check the mic
- ② LIGHT CHECK — calibrate the signal, not the content
- ③ TRASH TAKE — record to delete. Find your voice.
- Header: `3 TESTS — BUILD THE SYSTEM`

**④ `InfluencerVsSystem`** [1:40-2:00] — Strikethrough punch
- **INFLUENCER** dimmed + struck through → **SYSTEM** in gold
- Label: `THE GOAL ON DAY ONE`

---

Happy with these?

Done. Script 8 fully wired — 33 compositions total:

| File | Visual |
|---|---|
| `SixMonthsVsSixtySeconds` | 6 MONTHS struck through → 60 SECONDS (gold) — "YOUR FIRST VIDEO IS A TECHNICAL TEST" |
| `ProductiveFailure` | Coined term — PRODUCTIVE / FAILURE, fix: "UGLY FIRST → Better system faster." |
| `ThreeTests` | ① SYNC TEST ② LIGHT CHECK ③ TRASH TAKE — "3 TESTS — BUILD THE SYSTEM" |
| `InfluencerVsSystem` | INFLUENCER struck through → SYSTEM (gold) — "THE GOAL ON DAY ONE" |

To render Script 8: `npm run render:s8`

message.content:
Script: [0:00-0:15] Hook: "Do you ever feel like you’re making headway, only to realize four hours later you’re still obsessing over a single transition? It’s that 'Treadmill' loop where the more effort you put in, the less the math makes sense. You end up with a 'perfect' video that cost you £500 in lost billable time."
[0:15-0:40] Context: "A colleague spent all Sunday editing one clip. I told him: 'You’re over-engineering a bridge that only needs to carry a conversation.' He was addicted to the polish because he was scared the raw version wouldn't be enough."
[0:40-1:05] Logic: "The Law of Diminishing Returns hits founders hardest in the edit. The first hour gives you 80% of the value. After that, you're just 'faffing.' Viewers actually pull back from videos that feel too produced; they want a person, not an ad."
[1:05-1:40] The 3 Points: "Set three hard stops: 1. The Breath Cut Only—just remove the dead air. 2. The 10-Second Text Rule—only caption the 'Power Phrases.' 3. The Good Enough Timer—set a 60-minute alarm and hit 'export' when it dings."
[1:40-2:00] Closing: "When you stop over-editing, you start producing. I’ve pinned my 'Efficiency Edit' checklist at the top. See you in the next one."

content.text:
Here are my four picks:

---

**① `EditingTreadmill`** [0:00-0:15] — Coined term card
- **EDITING** (dark, slides in) + **TREADMILL** (steel blue, big bounce)
- Sub-line: *"More effort. Less return. Same spot."*

message.content:
Script: [0:00-0:15] Hook: "Do you ever feel like you’re making headway, only to realize your background looks like a spare bedroom? It’s a frustrating gap—knowing you’re an expert, but looking like you’re filming in a hallway. If you look disorganized, they’ll assume your process is disorganized too."
[0:15-0:40] Context: "A high-level coach was trying to sell a £5k program while sitting in front of a pile of laundry. I told him: 'Clients aren't just buying your brain; they’re buying the lifestyle they think you have.' You have to curate the 10% the camera sees."
[0:40-1:05] Logic: "This is the Halo Effect. If your environment looks high-end and orderly, people subconsciously assume your expertise is high-end, too. Visual order signals perceived competence. It makes the 'yes' happen before you even speak."
[1:05-1:40] The 3 Points: "1. The Depth Diagonal—angle your desk so the camera sees into the corner. 2. Zoned Lighting—light up one thing behind you, like a plant. 3. The Logic Props—remove clutter and replace it with two items that signal your niche."
[1:40-2:00] Closing: "When your background matches your pricing, sales get easier. I’ve pinned a 'Living Room Staging Blueprint' at the top. See you in the next one."

content.text:
Here are my four picks:

---

**① `HaloEffect`** [0:40-1:05] — Coined term card
- **HALO** (dark, slides in) + **EFFECT** (gold, big bounce)
- Sub-line: *"Visual order signals perceived competence"*
- Fix line: `CURATE IT → The yes happens before you speak.`

**② `TenPercent`** [0:15-0:40] — Hero stat
- Giant **10%** in gold
- Sub: `OF YOUR SPACE IS ALL THE CAMERA SEES`
- Label: `MAKE EVERY INCH COUNT`

**③ `ThreeBackgroundRules`** [1:05-1:40] — 3 rows stacking
- ① DEPTH DIAGONAL — angle desk into the corner
- ② ZONED LIGHTING — light one thing behind you
- ③ LOGIC PROPS — two items that signal your niche
- Header: `3 RULES — STAGE YOUR BACKGROUND`

**④ `DisorganizedVsCompetent`** [0:00-0:15] — Split comparison
- **DISORGANIZED** (dimmed) vs **COMPETENT** (gold)
- Sub-labels: `SPARE BEDROOM` vs `STAGED FRAME`
- Label: `YOUR BACKGROUND SPEAKS BEFORE YOU DO`

---

Happy with these?

content.text:
Done. Script 10 fully wired — 41 compositions total:

| File | Visual |
|---|---|
| `DisorganizedVsCompetent` | DISORGANIZED (dimmed) vs COMPETENT (gold) — "YOUR BACKGROUND SPEAKS BEFORE YOU DO" |
| `TenPercent` | Giant **10%** hero stat — "OF YOUR SPACE IS ALL THE CAMERA SEES" |
| `HaloEffect` | Coined term — HALO / EFFECT, fix: "CURATE IT → The yes happens before you speak." |
| `ThreeBackgroundRules` | ① DEPTH DIAGONAL ② ZONED LIGHTING ③ LOGIC PROPS |

To render Script 10: `npm run render:s10`

message.content: I am now moving onto my 5 minute scripts. I want to alter the way we design the infographics ever so slightly but I also don't want to lose the ability to generate the type of infographics we have been creating up to now.

content.text:
That makes sense — we can treat the existing style as the "short-form" template and build a new "long-form" template alongside it.

What changes did you have in mind for the 5-minute script graphics? For example:
- Different timing / pacing?
- More information on screen at once?
- A different visual style or layout?
- Something else entirely?

message.content: More information, slightly longer onscreen time, same layout and style, but maybe alter the transitions in and out. Maybe some of the text can break the 4th wall too.

content.text:
Great brief. Let me make sure I understand before building anything:

**More information** — more text per graphic, maybe an extra line or two beyond what the current cards show?

**Slightly longer onscreen** — what duration are you thinking? Something like 6–8 seconds total?

**Same layout and style** — same brand colours, Montserrat Extra Bold, same background gradient. ✓

**Alter the transitions in and out** — any direction you're thinking? For example:
- Slide in from the side rather than scale up
- Fade in flat rather than spring bounce
- A different dissolve out rather than the current opacity fade

**Break the 4th wall** — this is interesting. Do you mean things like:
- Text that speaks directly to the viewer ("Yes, this means you")
- A secondary line that adds a personal aside or commentary
- Something that feels like a whispered annotation on top of the main stat

The clearer you can get on these last two points the better — happy to suggest specific approaches too if you'd like some options.

message.content: 6 to 8 seconds total to accomodate more onscreen information to digest. Keep the transitions coming in and out the same way with the same fade out settings and same gradient fade out settings.I think text that speaks directly to the viewer is valuble, and having a secondary line as a personal aside as well as something that feels like a whispered annotation would be good. Unless that might be too much, what's your take on this?

content.text:
Honest take: all three on every card would be too much — it risks the card feeling cluttered and the viewer not knowing where to look first.

My suggestion is to use them selectively by card type:

**Coined term cards** (e.g. PREPARATION FATIGUE) — these suit a **personal aside** best. One quiet line that makes it feel like you're leaning in. *"I've seen this kill six-figure pipelines."*

**Stat/comparison cards** (e.g. 80% vs 20%) — these suit a **whispered annotation** best. A small italicised line that adds weight without competing. *"And most people don't notice until year two."*

**3-row list cards** — these already carry a lot of information. I'd leave them clean — no 4th wall text. The list does the talking.

**Strikethrough cards** (e.g. PERFECT → PROLIFIC) — these suit **direct address** best. One punchy line aimed straight at the viewer. *"This one's for you if you're still on draft four."*

So rather than three layers on every card, each card type gets **one** well-placed human moment. The result feels intentional rather than busy.

Does that framework feel right, or would you like to adjust which element goes where?

message.content: I think each has its own use case

content.text:
Agreed. So the plan is:

- **Coined term cards** → personal aside
- **Stat / comparison cards** → whispered annotation
- **Strikethrough cards** → direct address
- **3-row list cards** → left clean

The next step is a new `GraphicWrapperLong.tsx` that handles the longer duration (6–8 seconds) while keeping all the same transitions and gradient fade settings as `GraphicWrapper.tsx`. Each long-form composition will import from that instead.

Ready to proceed whenever you share the first 5-minute script. The 4th wall text can be passed in as props so each composition controls its own copy.

message.content:
Script: [0:00-0:45] BEAT 1: Roadmap: "It’s a weird feeling when you post a video, it gets more engagement than usual, and you feel like you’re finally getting somewhere—only to realize a week later that your actual business hasn't moved an inch. You feel like you're winning because the numbers on the screen are going up, but the reality is you’re just becoming a free teacher for a crowd that will never hire you. In the next five minutes, I want to show you why being 'helpful' to everyone is actually the reason your best work is being ignored by the people who could actually pay you. I’m going to show you why a video with 50 views is often worth more than one with 50,000, and how to stop talking like a hobbyist so the people who actually have a budget start paying attention. By the end, I’ll show you the three questions I use to make sure I’m not just wasting my own Tuesday afternoon filming stuff that doesn't matter."
[0:45-1:45] BEAT 2: Observation: "I spent a long time working with high-stakes teams—Real Estate agencies, professional Sports recruitment, even FOREX trading desks. And I noticed a massive gap between what they talk about and what everyone else talks about. In the trading world, beginners want to know 'how to read a chart.' But the pros? They don't care about that. They want to know about execution latency and risk models. I realized that by posting 'Top 5 Tips' for the general public, I was attracting people who just wanted to pick my brain for free. I was shouting into a stadium full of tourists while the three people in the back room who actually hold the keys were scrolling right past me because I sounded like a textbook. I had to realize that if I wanted to talk to an expert, I had to stop using the language of a beginner. That shift is the difference between being 'popular' and being 'respected.'"
[1:45-2:45] BEAT 3: Logic: "Think about your own business. If you’re a local lawyer or a tradesman, you don't need to be understood by everyone in the city. You need to be indispensable to the five people who have the specific problem you solve. When you try to be 'understood' by the general public, you use safe, generic language. But safe language is invisible to experts. Your ideal client is already overwhelmed with noise. They don't need more 'helpful' tips. They need a signal that says, 'I understand exactly why your specific situation is stuck right now.' You don't need more views. You need to be much louder to a much smaller group of people. If 95% of people find your video a bit boring or 'too technical,' that’s actually a good sign. It means you’re finally talking to the right 5%."
[2:45-3:45] BEAT 4: Shift: "The way to do this is to stop 'teaching' and start 'diagnosing.' Teaching is giving away a recipe; diagnosis is explaining why the kitchen is on fire. When you tell a business owner 'how' to do something, you're a resource they can find anywhere for free. But when you explain why their current way of doing things is quietly eating their time or their profit, you become the only person in the room who actually gets it. In Real Estate, it’s not about 'how to sell a house'; it’s about 'why your top agents are losing momentum after their first six months.' When you describe a problem better than the person experiencing it, they instinctively believe you have the solution. You haven't 'sold' them anything; you’ve just shown them that you’re already in the trenches with them. That’s where the trust shifts."
[3:45-4:45] BEAT 5: Asset Logic: "The goal here isn't to be 'famous.' It’s to build a library of your best thinking that does the work for you. When a prospect finds your work, they shouldn't just see a person 'making content.' They should see someone who has a consistent, logical way of solving a specific, expensive problem. It makes the actual conversation about working together a formality because you’ve already proven you understand the hidden frictions of their industry. You stop being a 'video guy' or a 'marketing guy' and you become a specialist. Whether you’re running a football team or a local accounting firm, the only way to scale is to be the person who speaks the language of the problem, not the language of the 'tip.'"
[4:45-5:00] BEAT 6: Closing: "If you want to see if your current videos are actually working or just taking up space, I’ve put a 'Specificity Audit' at the top of my profile. It’s just three questions that help you figure out if you're talking to the stadium or the room. I’ll see you in the next one."

content.text:
Great script. Before I propose the graphics, a quick note on the new long-form format:

- **Duration:** 210 frames (7 seconds) — 2s appear / 3.5s hold / 1.5s fade
- **Same transitions in and out** as the short-form
- **Same GraphicWrapper gradient fade** settings
- **New `GraphicWrapperLong.tsx`** — identical shell, just longer duration
- **4th wall text** passed as a prop, styled as a smaller secondary line beneath the main content

---

Here are my five picks across the six beats:

**① `FiftyVsFiftyThousand`** [Beat 1] — Stat comparison + whispered annotation
- **50** (gold) vs **50,000** (dimmed) — sub-labels: `VIEWS THAT CONVERT` vs `VIEWS THAT VANISH`
- Whispered: *"The smaller number is doing more work than you think."*

**② `PopularVsRespected`** [Beat 2] — Split comparison + whispered annotation
- **POPULAR** (dimmed) vs **RESPECTED** (gold) — sub-labels: `STADIUM OF TOURISTS` vs `ROOM THAT DECIDES`
- Whispered: *"You're probably optimising for the wrong one."*

**③ `RightFivePercent`** [Beat 3] — Hero stat + whispered annotation
- Giant **5%** in gold — sub: `OF YOUR AUDIENCE IS ALL YOU NEED`
- Whispered: *"The other 95% were never going to hire you."*

**④ `TeachingVsDiagnosing`** [Beat 4] — Strikethrough + direct address
- **TEACHING** struck through → **DIAGNOSING** (gold)
- Direct address: *"Which one are you doing right now?"*

**⑤ `ThreeAuditQuestions`** [Beat 6] — 3-row list, left clean
- ① Does this speak to a specific problem?
- ② Would a beginner understand it? (bad sign)
- ③ Does it make them feel seen?
- Header: `THE SPECIFICITY AUDIT`

---

Happy with these?

content.text:
Good thinking. Two parts to this answer:

---

**First — back up the project before you upgrade.**

The code itself is the most important thing. Push it to a free private GitHub repo:

```bash
cd C:\Users\mcopp\focus-remotion
git init
git add .
git commit -m "full project backup"
```
Then create a repo on github.com and push to it. After your SSD upgrade, clone it back in one command. Also copy `C:\Users\mcopp\CLAUDE.md` and `C:\Users\mcopp\.claude` to an external drive — those are your memory and brand guidelines files.

---

**Second — if you do lose everything, use this prompt:**

```
I have a Remotion motion graphics project I need to rebuild. 
Please set up the project from scratch at C:\Users\mcopp\focus-remotion 
using the following brand system:

BRAND TOKENS:
- Background: radial-gradient(ellipse at 50% 50%, #e0e1dd 0%, #e0e1dd 20%, #6b6b6b 60%, #222222 100%)
- ringPrimary: #415a77 (steel blue — the "old way", dimmed elements)
- ringSecondary: #c2a46d (warm gold — the "goal", hero elements)
- textPrimary: #1D1D1F, textSecondary: rgba(29,29,31,0.45)
- textMuted: rgba(29,29,31,0.6), bgCenter: #e0e1dd
- Font: Montserrat Extra Bold (800) for all text — no exceptions
- Springs: snappy { damping:15, stiffness:220 }, bouncy { damping:12, stiffness:280 }, smooth { damping:14, stiffness:200 }

ANIMATION SYSTEM:
- All tokens live in src/brand.ts
- GraphicWrapper.tsx — shared shell for SHORT-FORM compositions (2-minute scripts):
  * Spring scale-in (0.84→1) + opacity fade-in (frames 0–8)
  * Content + gradient fade out together over 7 frames, then flat background clears over 8 frames
  * dissolveStart = durationInFrames - 15 (0.5s before end)
  * NO blur — opacity-only dissolve to avoid colour banding
  * Gradient on a separate overlay div, fades with content
- GraphicWrapperLong.tsx — shell for LONG-FORM compositions (5-minute scripts):
  * Identical to GraphicWrapper but with optional `footnote` prop
  * Footnote = whispered annotation, fades in at frame 55–70, positioned at bottom: 220
  * Styled italic, fontSize 15, COLOR.textMuted, prefixed with "— "

COMPOSITION TYPES (short-form, durationInFrames=120):
1. Coined term cards — two-line stacked term (e.g. PREPARATION / FATIGUE), sub-line description, horizontal rule, fix line with arrow
2. Split comparison cards — LEFT (dimmed ringPrimary, the problem) vs RIGHT (gold, the goal), vertical divider, bottom label
3. Strikethrough cards — TOP word dimmed + animated strikethrough draws left→right, down arrow, BOTTOM word in gold, label
4. Three-row list cards — header label, 3 rows slide in from left with staggered delays [8, 36, 64], each row: number badge (gold) + category (steel blue) + arrow + fix text + detail text

COMPOSITION TYPES (long-form, durationInFrames=210):
- Same 4 types but using GraphicWrapperLong
- Stat/comparison cards get a whispered annotation footnote
- Strikethrough cards get a direct address line (gold italic, fades in at frame 58–70) inside the composition JSX
- Three-row list cards are left clean

RENDER CONVENTIONS:
- Output to out/ folder, prefixed s1- through s10- for short scripts, ls1- onwards for long scripts
- WebM with --pixel-format yuva420p for transparency
- ProRes 4444 .mov as alternative
- Render scripts in package.json grouped as render:s1 through render:s10, render:ls1 etc.
- render:all chains all scripts

I have 10 completed short-form scripts (s1–s10, 41 compositions) and 1 long-form script (ls1, 5 compositions). 
Please rebuild the project structure, install dependencies, and then I will feed you each script to rebuild the compositions.
```

---

That prompt gives a fresh Claude everything it needs to rebuild the entire system from scratch. But GitHub is the safer bet — takes 5 minutes and means you don't have to rebuild anything.

content.text:
<summary>
1. Primary Request and Intent:
The user is a content creator/founder building a comprehensive library of motion graphics in Remotion (React-based video framework) to overlay on video content. The work spans:
- Building short-form motion graphics (2-min scripts, 120 frames = 4s total) for 10 scripts
- Building long-form motion graphics (5-min scripts, 210 frames = 7s total) with 4th wall text
- Iterative timing adjustments throughout
- Fixing colour banding on dissolve-out
- Creating a new GraphicWrapperLong for long-form content
- Asking how to back up the project to GitHub before an SSD upgrade

2. Key Technical Concepts:
- Remotion v4 (React/TypeScript video framework)
- Transparent video export: WebM yuva420p and ProRes 4444
- Brand token system in brand.ts (colours, springs, font weights)
- GraphicWrapper.tsx — shared shell (short-form): spring scale-in, opacity-only dissolve (no blur), gradient on separate overlay layer
- GraphicWrapperLong.tsx — long-form shell: identical to GraphicWrapper + optional `footnote` prop (whispered annotation at bottom: 220)
- Colour banding fix: removed Gaussian blur entirely; content + gradient fade via opacity together, flat background clears over 8 frames
- 4th wall text system: coined term cards → personal aside; stat/comparison → whispered annotation (footnote prop); strikethrough → direct address (in JSX); 3-row lists → left clean
- Composition types: coined term, split comparison, strikethrough, three-row list, hero stat
- Spring configs: snappy {damping:15, stiffness:220}, bouncy {damping:12, stiffness:280}, smooth {damping:14, stiffness:200}
- Render script naming: s1–s10 (short), ls1 (long), with WebM and ProRes variants
- ROW_DELAYS = [8, 36, 64] for three-row compositions

3. Files and Code Sections:

- `src/brand.ts`
  - Single source of truth for all brand tokens
  - COLOR, BG_GRADIENT, FONT_WEIGHT, SPRING, RING constants

- `src/GraphicWrapper.tsx`
  - Short-form shell. Key: opacity-only dissolve (no blur to avoid banding), gradient on separate overlay div that fades with content, flat bgCenter background clears over 8 frames after
  - dissolveStart = durationInFrames - 15 (0.5s)
  - contentOp fades [dissolveStart, dissolveStart+7], bgOp fades [dissolveStart+7, dissolveStart+15]

- `src/GraphicWrapperLong.tsx`
  - Long-form shell, identical transitions to GraphicWrapper
  - Adds optional `footnote?: string` prop
  - Footnote: position absolute, bottom: 220, fades in at frames [55,70], italic, fontSize 15, COLOR.textMuted, prefixed "— "
  - Full code:
```tsx
interface Props {
  children: React.ReactNode;
  footnote?: string;
}
export const GraphicWrapperLong: React.FC<Props> = ({ children, footnote }) => {
  const dissolveStart = durationInFrames - 15;
  const contentOp = interpolate(frame, [dissolveStart, dissolveStart + 7], [1, 0], { extrapolateLeft: "clamp", extrapolateRight: "clamp" });
  const bgOp = interpolate(frame, [dissolveStart + 7, dissolveStart + 15], [1, 0], { extrapolateLeft: "clamp", extrapolateRight: "clamp" });
  const footnoteOp = interpolate(frame, [55, 70], [0, 1], { extrapolateLeft: "clamp", extrapolateRight: "clamp" });
  // footnote positioned at bottom: 220
```

- `src/Root.tsx`
  - Registers all 46 compositions across 10 short scripts + 1 long script
  - Short-form: durationInFrames={120}, Long-form: durationInFrames={210}
  - All at fps={30} width={1920} height={1080}

- `package.json`
  - render:all chains s1–s10 and ls1
  - render:prores:all chains same
  - Individual render commands per composition
  - ls1 render commands use ls1- prefix in output filenames

- Short-form compositions (Scripts 1–10, 41 total):
  - S1: FocusPie, FourHoursCost, TwentyThreeMin, SwitchingCost, NinetyDays
  - S2: FiveKvsPhysics, InterrogationShadows, ThreeFixes, HundredBeats
  - S3: PerceivedTruth, ListenerFatigue, ThreeAudioFixes, ThreeMicsUnder50
  - S4: PitchVsPartnership, AnchorPoints, ThreeTechniques, PerfectVsProlific
  - S5: PreparationFatigue, ManagerVsCreator, SetupTax, ThreeBatchRules
  - S6: HobbyistVsAuthority, SignalStrength, FlatVsDimensional, ThreeLights
  - S7: HighChairEffect, PermissionVsDirection, AuthoritySignal, ThreeFramingRules
  - S8: SixMonthsVsSixtySeconds, ProductiveFailure, ThreeTests, InfluencerVsSystem
  - S9: EditingTreadmill, EightyPercent, ThreeEditRules, OverEditingVsProducing
  - S10: DisorganizedVsCompetent, TenPercent, HaloEffect, ThreeBackgroundRules

- Long-form compositions (Long Script 1, 5 total):
  - FiftyVsFiftyThousand — stat comparison, footnote: "The smaller number is doing more work than you think."
  - PopularVsRespected — split comparison, footnote: "You're probably optimising for the wrong one."
  - RightFivePercent — hero stat, footnote: "The other 95% were never going to hire you."
  - TeachingVsDiagnosing — strikethrough, direct address: "Which one are you doing right now?" (in JSX, gold italic, fades frames 58–70)
  - ThreeAuditQuestions — 3-row list, clean (no footnote)

- `C:\Users\mcopp\CLAUDE.md`
  - Brand guidelines auto-loaded each session
  - All colour tokens, typography rules, animation style

- `C:\Users\mcopp\.claude\projects\C--Users-mcopp\memory\brand_guidelines.md`
  - Persistent cross-session memory of brand system

4. Errors and fixes:
- **Colour banding on gradient during dissolve-out**: Gaussian blur applied to radial gradient caused harsh banding. Fix: removed blur entirely. Content + gradient fade out together via opacity. Flat bgCenter background then clears over 8 frames. No blur = no banding.
- **Footnote text too low on screen (masked by dark gradient)**: Initially at bottom: 64, then raised to bottom: 160 (still too low), then raised to bottom: 220 (accepted by user).
- **Edit tool string not found error**: When editing Root.tsx with whitespace mismatches. Fixed by reading file first to get exact string.
- **ROW_DELAYS conflict**: When reducing all compositions to 120 frames, 3-row comps had delays [8, 52, 96] with last row appearing at dissolveStart. Fixed by tightening to [8, 36, 64].

5. Problem Solving:
- **Banding fix**: Decoupled gradient and blur into separate opacity phases. Gradient fades with content, then flat background clears. No blur used anywhere.
- **Duration system**: Single durationInFrames value in Root.tsx controls all timing. GraphicWrapper calculates dissolveStart dynamically from durationInFrames. Changing duration in one place affects all compositions.
- **Long-form 4th wall text**: Decided each card type gets exactly one human moment rather than stacking all three approaches. Footnote in wrapper handles whispered annotations; direct address rendered in composition JSX for strikethrough cards; 3-row lists left clean.
- **Footnote positioning**: Moved from bottom: 64 → 160 → 220 to escape dark outer gradient ring.

6. All user messages:
- "Can we readjust so that each clip 4 seconds long instead, and can we have the gradient disappear 0.1 seconds before the rest of the graphic begins reducing opacity"
- "Actually, let's have it hold for 1.5 seconds"
- "I am experiencing the same problem with the onscreen graphics as I did with the gradient. the colour banding is very strong. Can those also fade at the same time as the gradient. The entire image should become fully transparent after that over 8 frames."
- "Can I supply another script please?" (×multiple)
- Script pastes for Scripts 5–10 and Long Script 1
- "Yes please" / "Yes" / "Yes I am" / "Yes go ahead" (approvals to build)
- "Perfect, lets work on the next script please?"
- "I am now moving onto my 5 minute scripts. I want to alter the way we design the infographics ever so slightly but I also don't want to lose the ability to generate the type of infographics we have been creating up to now."
- "More information, slightly longer onscreen time, same layout and style, but maybe alter the transitions in and out. Maybe some of the text can break the 4th wall too."
- "6 to 8 seconds total to accommodate more onscreen information to digest. Keep the transitions coming in and out the same way with the same fade out settings and same gradient fade out settings. I think text that speaks directly to the viewer is valuable, and having a secondary line as a personal aside as well as something that feels like a whispered annotation would be good. Unless that might be too much, what's your take on this?"
- "I think each has its own use case"
- Long Script 1 paste (5-minute Specificity script)
- "Yes" (approval to build ls1 compositions)
- "I can see text that appears a little too low on the screen. It starts to become masked by the gradient a little and it makes it difficult to read it."
- "It is still a little too low. Can we raise it a bit more?"
- "Perfect."
- "I am going to upgrade my SSD soon. When I do, I will likely have to reinstall Claude. What you have saved to my local drive will no longer be there and you will no longer be able to remember what infographics I am looking for. Everything from brand design, colour, theme, style, and font. What prompt must I use so that when I reinstall Claude you are able to continue rendering files like this one as well as the ones from scripts 1-10 precisely?"
- "How do I do it with the github?"

7. Pending Tasks:
- Answer the user's question about how to use GitHub to back up the project (this was the last unanswered question before the summary request)

8. Current Work:
The user asked "How do I do it with the github?" immediately before the summary request was triggered. The assistant had just provided:
1. A recommendation to back up to GitHub before SSD upgrade
2. Git commands to initialise and commit the project
3. A comprehensive fallback prompt for rebuilding from scratch

The user wants step-by-step GitHub instructions — how to create a repo, push the code, and restore it after the SSD upgrade.

9. Optional Next Step:
Provide clear step-by-step instructions for backing up the project to GitHub. The user asked "How do I do it with the github?" in direct response to the recommendation to push to a private GitHub repo. This should cover: creating a GitHub account/repo, pushing the local project, and restoring after SSD upgrade.
</summary>
